persona.name: Zoe Park

occupation.title: Pre-Med Student / Dermatology Lab Research Assistant
occupation.description: Full-time pre-med junior at UCLA majoring in Biology, working 10 hours/week as a research assistant in a dermatology lab where she processes skin biopsy samples and assists graduate students. Balances a heavy course load with MCAT prep and lab duties. Interested in specializing in dermatology after medical school.

persona.education: Junior at UCLA majoring in Biology with a Chemistry minor. Graduated top of her class from Arcadia High School in the San Gabriel Valley. Plans to apply to medical school with an interest in dermatology.
long_term_goals: Get accepted to a top-tier medical school and specialize in dermatology
Publish a research paper on skin microbiome before graduating
Help her immigrant parents achieve financial security once she has a career
Travel to South Korea to learn more about her heritage
Build a sustainable, scientifically validated skincare routine as a daily practice
persona.style: Zoe communicates in rapid-fire bursts — she gives three supporting examples before you've finished asking the question. Her vocabulary blends pre-med precision ('ceramide-depleted lipid barrier') with Gen Z social media shorthand. She's put-together in a subtle K-beauty way: tinted SPF, slight gloss, clean minimalist aesthetic. She gestures a lot when excited and raises one eyebrow when skeptical while fact-checking on her phone. Will confidently correct bad skincare advice in any setting.

traits: Analytically curious — constantly researching ingredient lists and clinical studies
Mildly perfectionistic about her routine; gets anxious when she breaks out
Loyal to brands once she trusts them, slow to adopt new ones without evidence
Competitive academically but generous about sharing skincare tips with friends
Will justify premium purchases as 'preventive dermatology investments'
Prone to over-googling skin symptoms and catastrophizing minor flare-ups

interests: Skincare science and cosmetic chemistry (Dr. Dray, Hyram, and r/SkincareAddiction)
K-beauty trends and ingredient deep-dives on YouTube
Dermatology research, especially skin microbiome studies
MCAT prep and academic performance optimization
Korean drama and webtoons for stress relief
Budget meal-prepping to save money for skincare
likes: Niacinamide serums for her uneven skin tone
Products with clear clinical backing and percentages on packaging
Affordable drugstore dupes for luxury actives
Full INCI lists with disclosed concentrations
H-Mart skincare hauls with her mom
Boba from the San Gabriel Valley
dislikes: Fragrance in skincare — triggers sensitivity and inflammation
Brands that don't disclose ingredient percentages
Influencers who push products without clinical evidence
Mineral sunscreens that leave a white cast on her skin tone
Vague 'natural' claims without specific safety or efficacy data

beliefs: Good skin is science plus genetics plus consistency — not luck
You should understand what you're putting on your face before using it
Accessible skincare pricing is a social equity issue
Career success requires sacrificing short-term comfort for long-term goals
Cultural heritage should be honored, not erased, while assimilating
skills: Ingredient safety assessment using EWG and CosDNA
PubMed literature review for cosmetic dermatology studies
Budget optimization — identifying best-value drugstore alternatives to luxury actives
Lab technique: microscopy and sample preparation

general: Photographs her skin under natural light weekly to track progress
Screenshots TikTok recommendations, then cross-references on r/SkincareAddiction before buying
Carries SPF in her backpack and reapplies at noon
Spends $60–$100/month on skincare and justifies it as preventive care
Will ghost a brand after one bad reformulation

morning: 5:45am wake-up, MCAT flashcards while eating yogurt
Micellar water cleanse (double cleanse only if heavy SPF prior night)
The Ordinary Vitamin C 23% serum, moisturizer, SPF 50+
workday: Check skincare TikTok DMs between classes
evening: Oil cleanser + gentle foam double cleanse
Tretinoin 0.025% Mon/Wed/Fri (prescribed by UCLA dermatology clinic)
Sheet mask on weekends while watching K-drama
weekend: H-Mart run with her mom for Korean skincare hauls
Studies at a café in Koreatown
Face masks with her roommate while watching reality TV

health.physical: Generally healthy but manages mild hormonal acne along her jawline that flares with stress. Sensitive skin reacts to fragrance and alcohol-heavy formulas. Uses prescription tretinoin. Runs 3x/week on the UCLA track.
health.mental: High-functioning anxiety that keeps her productive but occasionally tips into overwhelm during finals. Managing with exercise and sleep consistency. Sees a counselor at UCLA monthly.

relationships.name: Mrs. Park
relationships.description: Immigrated from South Korea in 1999; works as a nail technician in Arcadia. First introduced Zoe to Korean skincare and still sends her Cosrx products. Zoe calls her every Sunday.

relationships.name: Jennifer Kim
relationships.description: Also pre-med at UCLA. They study together, swap product samples, and hold each other accountable on sleep schedules. Friendly competition over GPA.

relationships.name: Dr. Nguyen
relationships.description: Vietnamese-American professor who supervises her research and is writing her med school recommendation. A key mentor.
